xtd::convert Class Reference
﹡ libraries » xtd.core | system
Inheritance diagram for xtd::convert:

Definition

Represents API to convert base type code.

Header
#include <xtd/convert>
Namespace
xtd
Library
xtd.core
Remarks
This class cannot be inherited.

Public Static Methods

static auto from_base64_string (const xtd::string &s) -> xtd::array< xtd::byte >
 Converts the specified string, which encodes binary data as base-64 digits, to an equivalent 8-bit unsigned integer array.

static auto to_base64_string (const xtd::array< xtd::byte > &in_array) -> xtd::string
 Converts an array of 8-bit unsigned integers to its equivalent string representation that is encoded with base-64 digits.
static auto to_base64_string (const xtd::read_only_span< xtd::byte > &bytes) -> xtd::string
 Converts a read only span of 8-bit unsigned integers to its equivalent string representation that is encoded with base-64 digits.

Member Function Documentation

◆ from_base64_string()

auto xtd::convert::from_base64_string ( const xtd::string & s) -> xtd::array< xtd::byte >
staticnodiscard

Converts the specified string, which encodes binary data as base-64 digits, to an equivalent 8-bit unsigned integer array.

Parameters
sThe string to convert.
Returns
An array of 8-bit unsigned integers that is equivalent to s.
Exceptions
xtd::format_exceptionThe length of `s`, is not zero or a multiple of 4.
Remarks
s is composed of base-64 digits, white-space characters, and trailing padding characters. The base-64 digits in ascending order from zero are the uppercase characters "A" to "Z", lowercase characters "a" to "z", numerals "0" to "9", and the symbols "+" and "/".
The white-space characters, and their Unicode names and hexadecimal code points, are tab (CHARACTER TABULATION, U+0009), newline (LINE FEED, U+000A), carriage return (CARRIAGE RETURN, U+000D), and blank (SPACE, U+0020). An arbitrary number of white-space characters can appear in s because all white-space characters are ignored.
The valueless character, "=", is used for trailing padding. The end of s can consist of zero, one, or two padding characters.

◆ to_any() [1/22]

auto xtd::convert::to_any ( xtd::any value) -> xtd::any
staticnodiscardnoexcept

Convert xtd::any to xtd::any.

Parameters
valueobject to convert.
Returns
A new xtd::any object converted from value.
Examples
xtd::any value = true;
any result = convert::to_any(value);
xtd::convert::to_any
static auto to_any(xtd::any value) noexcept -> xtd::any
Convert xtd::any to xtd::any.
xtd::any
std::any any
Represents the any alias on std::any.

staticnodiscard

Parameters

The string representation, in base 64, of the contents of in_array.

valueobject to convert.

uint64t value = 42ul;
xtd::byte result = convert::to_byte(value);

valueobject to convert.
from_baseThe base of the number in value, which must be 2, 8, 10, or 16.

◆ to_char16() [15/22]

auto xtd::convert::to_char16 ( slong value) -> xtd::char16
staticnodiscard

Convert slong to char16.

Parameters
valueobject to convert.
Returns
A new char16 object converted from value.
Exceptions
xtd::overflow_exceptionvalue represents a number that is greater than std::numric_limit<xtd::byte>::max() or is less than 0.
Examples
slong value = 42ll;
char16 result = convert::to_char16(value);

staticnodiscardnoexcept

Convert int32 to decimal.

Parameters
valueobject to convert.
Returns
A new decimal object converted from value.
Examples
int32 value = 42;
decimal result = convert::to_decimal(value);

auto xtd::convert::to_int16 ( char32 value) -> xtd::int16
staticnodiscard

Convert char32 to int16.

Parameters
valueobject to convert.
Returns
A new int16 object converted from value.
Exceptions
xtd::overflow_exceptionvalue represents a number that is greater than std::numric_limit<xtd::byte>::max() or is less than 0.
Examples
char32 value = U'a';
int16 result = convert::to_int16(value);

valueobject to convert.

xtd::overflow_exceptionvalue represents a number that is greater than std::numric_limit<xtd::byte>::max() or is less than 0.

A new int16 object converted from value.
Examples
decimal value = 42.50l;

Returns
A new int16 object converted from value.
Exceptions
xtd::overflow_exceptionvalue represents a number that is greater than std::numric_limit<xtd::byte>::max().
Examples
xtd::ulong value = 42ull;
int16 result = convert::to_int16(value);

◆ to_int16() [21/23]

auto xtd::convert::to_int16 ( const xtd::string & value) -> xtd::int16
staticnodiscard

Convert string to int16.

Parameters
valueobject to convert.
Returns
A new int16 object converted from value.
Exceptions
xtd::atgument_exceptionvalue does not represent a byte.
Examples
string value = "42";
int16 result = convert::to_int16(value);

◆ to_int16() [22/23]

Parameters
valueobject to convert.
from_baseThe base of the number in value, which must be 2, 8, 10, or 16.
Returns
A new intt16_t object converted from value.

Examples

staticnodiscard

Convert decimal to int32.

valueobject to convert.
Remarks
The result is rounded.

xtd::overflow_exceptionvalue represents a number that is greater than std::numric_limit<xtd::byte>::max() or is less than 0.

A new int32 object converted from value.
Examples
decimal value = 42.50l;

uint32 value = 42u;
slong result = convert::to_llong(value);

Parameters
valueobject to convert.
Returns
A new uint32 object converted from value.
Remarks
The result is rounded.
Exceptions
xtd::overflow_exceptionvalue represents a number that is greater than std::numric_limit<xtd::byte>::max() or is less than 0.
Examples
double value = 42.50;
uint32 result = convert::to_uint32(value);

◆ to_uint32() [11/23]

auto xtd::convert::to_uint32 ( float value) -> xtd::uint32
staticnodiscard

Convert float to uint32.

Parameters
valueobject to convert.
Returns
A new uint32 object converted from value.
Exceptions
xtd::overflow_exceptionvalue represents a number that is greater than std::numric_limit<xtd::byte>::max() or is less than 0.
Remarks
The result is rounded.
Examples
float value = 42.50f;
uint32 result = convert::to_uint32(value);

valueobject to convert.
from_baseThe base of the number in value, which must be 2, 8, 10, or 16.

A new uintt32_t object converted from value.

xtd::atgument_exceptionvalue does not represent a byte.
Examples
string value = "42";

auto xtd::convert::to_string ( xtd::any value) -> xtd::string
staticnodiscard

Convert xtd::any to string.

Parameters
valueobject to convert.
Returns
A new string object converted from value.
Examples
xtd::any value = 42;
string result = convert::to_string(value);
xtd::convert::to_string
static auto to_string(xtd::any value) -> xtd::string
Convert xtd::any to string.

valueobject to convert.
Returns

Examples

auto xtd::convert::to_string ( xtd::byte value) -> xtd::string
staticnodiscardnoexcept

Convert xtd::byte to string.

Parameters
valueobject to convert.
Returns
A new string object converted from value.
Examples

string result = convert::to_string(value);

◆ to_string() [4/32]

auto xtd::convert::to_string ( xtd::byte value,
xtd::byte from_base ) -> xtd::string
staticnodiscard

Convert string to string.

Parameters
valueobject to convert.
from_baseThe base of the number in value, which must be 2, 8, 10, or 16.
Returns
A new string object converted from value.
Exceptions
xtd::atgument_exceptionfrom_base is not 2 or 8 or 10 orr 16.
Examples
xtd::byte value = 42;
string result = convert::to_string(value, 16);

◆ to_string() [10/32]

auto xtd::convert::to_string ( decimal value) -> xtd::string
staticnodiscard

Convert decimal to string.

Parameters
valueobject to convert.
Remarks
The result is rounded.
Exceptions
xtd::overflow_exceptionvalue represents a number that is greater than std::numric_limit<xtd::byte>::max() or is less than 0.
Returns
A new string object converted from value.
Examples
decimal value = 42.50l;
string result = convert::to_string(value);

◆ to_string() [11/32]

valueobject to convert.
Returns
A new string object converted from value.
Remarks
The result is rounded.
Exceptions

Examples
double value = 42.50;
string result = convert::to_string(value);

◆ to_string() [12/32]

auto xtd::convert::to_string ( float value) -> xtd::string
staticnodiscard

Convert float to string.

Parameters
valueobject to convert.
Returns
A new string object converted from value.
Exceptions
xtd::overflow_exceptionvalue represents a number that is greater than std::numric_limit<xtd::byte>::max() or is less than 0.
Remarks
The result is rounded.
Examples
float value = 42.50f;
string result = convert::to_string(value);
